$description: Single source of truth for the storage-graph node. Consumed by both the backend executor (provider/mode/credential validation) and the frontend GraphDatabaseSettingsPanel (provider picker UI). Add a provider here and it appears everywhere — no code change required.

providers.label: Neo4j
providers.description: Cypher-based property graph. AuraDB managed or self-hosted.

providers.label: Memgraph
providers.description: Cypher-compatible, in-memory graph store with streaming.

providers.label: NebulaGraph
providers.description: Distributed billion-edge graph DB with nGQL.

providers.label: ArangoDB
providers.description: Document + graph + key-value in one engine, AQL queries.

providers.label: AWS Neptune
providers.description: Managed graph DB supporting both Gremlin and SPARQL.

providers.label: Kùzu
providers.description: Embeddable analytical graph DB, runs in-process.

providers.label: NetworkX
providers.description: Pure-Python in-memory graph. Great for prototyping.

extractorStrategies.label: LLM-based entity & relation extraction
extractorStrategies.description: Uses the connected LLM to extract (subject, predicate, object) triples from each chunk. Highest quality, highest cost.

extractorStrategies.label: spaCy NER + dependency parse
extractorStrategies.description: Local statistical NER + verb-based relation heuristics. Fast and cheap.

extractorStrategies.label: REBEL (end-to-end relation extraction)
extractorStrategies.description: BART-based seq2seq relation extractor. Good middle ground.

extractorStrategies.label: Pre-extracted (chunks contain triples)
extractorStrategies.description: Skip extraction — assumes upstream node already produced graph triples.
